segments.text:
Primary Imunodeficiency
-genetic, or developmental defect
Secondary Immunodeficiency
- an acquired defect

SCID
Severe Combined ImmunoDeficiency, a genetic defect in which adaptive immune responses do not occur because of a lack of T cells and possibly B and NK cells.
Most Primary Immunodeficiencies are Congenital
Distribution of genetic defects in 170 cases of SCID tracked over 35 years

SCID-ADA
Mutation in Adenosine Deaminase gene
Immature T and B cells die due to toxic levels of deoxyadenosine and dATP (which are produced from adenosine)
It has been known for a long time that adenosine is an immunosuppressor
Adenosine                                     Inosine
ADA

Germline Therapy
-use eggs, sperm, fertilized egg
-not in current use
-not recommended
by scientist
-successfully used    	in animals

Somatic Cell Gene Therapy
Remove cells from individual
Insert DNA
Return cells
or
Inject DNA directly into individual

Stem cell isolation:
Sources
Cord blood
Bone marrow
Peripheral blood (after mobilizing cells with 	compounds like granulocyte colony 	stimulating factor G-CSF)

Bone marrow
Peripheral
Blood

Stem cell isolation
Columns with monoclonal antibody to CD34

Moloney retrovirus
Genetically Modified Moloney Retrovirus
Therapeutic Gene (TG) to be inserted into somatic cell
ADA
Empty virus factory plus Therapeutic Gene
This cell line produces infectious virus that carries desired gene

First success
Two young girls with SCID
Lymphcytes removed
Cells infected with virus containing 	cDNA for ADA
Cells re-introduced
Symptoms gone
Jesse Gelsinger 1981-1999
Interleukin-2 (T cell mitogen)
Interleukin-2 shares a common binding receptor with other interluekins

Allogenic-transplant from a different person
Autogenic-self transplant
Standard treatment is cell transplants from HLA-matched patients

-Disease: SCID –X1
-X linked gene codes for  cytokine receptor
-used Moloney Virus
-used hematopoietic stem cells

2019
CRISPR can be used to eliminate off target insertions
Guide RNA
